annotations.label: Tensor product of two vector spaces

anchor.snippet: the tensor product

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `TensorProduct R M N` for modules over a commutative semiring, which subsumes the vector-space case.

annotations.label: Elementary/decomposable tensor

anchor.snippet: is called the tensor product of

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.tmul` (notation `m ⊗ₜ n`) is the elementary/decomposable tensor.

annotations.label: Universal property of bilinear maps

anchor.snippet: captures the properties of all bilinear maps

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.lift.equiv` gives the linear equivalence between bilinear maps and linear maps out of the tensor product.

annotations.label: Metric tensor in general relativity

anchor.snippet: in general relativity

annotations.note: Physics example not covered in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tensor product from bases

anchor.snippet: is a vector space that has as a basis the set of all

annotations.note: `Basis.tensorProduct` builds a basis of `M ⊗ N` from bases of `M` and `N`.

annotations.label: Tensor product as space of bilinear forms

anchor.snippet: we can define

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses the dual view through the universal property/lift equiv rather than as a definitional construction from bilinear forms.

annotations.label: Tensor product of vectors via basis decomposition

anchor.snippet: the tensor product of two vectors is defined from their decomposition on the bases

annotations.note: `Basis.tensorProduct_apply` describes how `v ⊗ w` expands in the product basis.

annotations.label: Tensor product as generalization of outer product

anchor.snippet: the coordinate vector of

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Matrix.kroneckerTMul` realizes the coordinate-level outer-product description, though the article's exact phrasing isn't a dedicated lemma.

annotations.label: Tensor product as a quotient space

anchor.snippet: the tensor product is defined as the quotient space

annotations.note: `TensorProduct` is defined as a quotient of the free additive monoid by the bilinearity relation (`AddCon.ker`).

annotations.label: Universal property of the tensor product

anchor.snippet: The tensor product of two vector spaces V and W is a vector space denoted as

annotations.note: Encoded as the equivalence between bilinear maps and linear maps from the tensor product.

annotations.label: Tensor product of F^m and F^n

anchor.snippet: where

annotations.note: Not stated as a standalone identification but follows from the dimension formula and basis lemmas.

annotations.label: Tensor product of function spaces

anchor.snippet: is the vector space of all complex-valued functions on a set

annotations.note: No identification of `C(S) ⊗ C(T) ↪ C(S×T)` as such is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characterization via linear disjointness
annotations.note: `Submodule.LinearDisjoint` is defined exactly as injectivity of the canonical map `M ⊗ N → S`.

annotations.label: Dimension of tensor product

anchor.snippet: is finite-dimensional, and its dimension is the product of the dimensions

annotations.note: `Module.finrank_tensorProduct` states `finrank(M ⊗ N) = finrank M * finrank N`.

annotations.label: Associativity of tensor product

anchor.snippet: The tensor product is associative in the sense that

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.assoc` gives the canonical linear equivalence `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P ≃ M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.

annotations.label: Commutativity of tensor product of spaces

anchor.snippet: is commutative in the sense that there is a canonical isomorphism

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.comm` is the canonical linear equivalence `M ⊗ N ≃ N ⊗ M`.

annotations.label: Braiding map

anchor.snippet: induces a linear automorphism that is called a braiding map

annotations.note: The braiding `v ⊗ w ↦ w ⊗ v` is realized by `TensorProduct.comm` (also packaged categorically via monoidal braidings).

annotations.label: Tensor product of linear maps

anchor.snippet: is the unique linear map such that

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.map f g` is the tensor product of two linear maps.

annotations.label: Tensor product of two linear maps f and g

anchor.snippet: their tensor product

annotations.note: Same construction `TensorProduct.map f g : M ⊗ N →ₗ M' ⊗ N'`.

annotations.label: Tensor product is a bifunctor

anchor.snippet: this means that the tensor product is a bifunctor

annotations.note: The monoidal-category structure on `ModuleCat R` encodes the bifunctorial behavior of tensor product.

annotations.label: Tensor product preserves injectivity/surjectivity; exactness

anchor.snippet: are both injective or surjective

annotations.note: Surjectivity preservation is `TensorProduct.map_surjective`/`LinearMap.rTensor_surjective`; injectivity requires flatness in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Matrix of tensor product is Kronecker product

anchor.snippet: the matrix describing the tensor product

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.toMatrix_map` shows the matrix of `TensorProduct.map f g` equals the Kronecker product `kroneckerMap` of the matrices of `f` and `g`.

annotations.label: Dyadic product

anchor.snippet: A dyadic product is the special case of the tensor product

annotations.note: Mathlib has elementary tensors `m ⊗ₜ n` but no separate `dyadic` term; this is just a vector outer product, a special case of `tmul`.

annotations.label: Type (r,s) tensor

anchor.snippet: tensor on a vector space

annotations.note: Mathlib has `PiTensorProduct` for arbitrary tensor powers; the typed `V^{⊗r} ⊗ (V*)^{⊗s}` formulation is not a dedicated definition.

annotations.label: Tensor product of tensors

anchor.snippet: There is a product map, called the (tensor) product of tensors

annotations.note: Multiplication in `TensorAlgebra` (and `TensorProduct.map`) realizes the product of tensors.

annotations.label: Components of product of covariant tensors

anchor.snippet: if F and G are two covariant tensors of orders

annotations.note: No componentwise multiplication formula for covariant tensors is recorded.

annotations.label: Tensor algebra

anchor.snippet: Tensors equipped with their product operation form an algebra

annotations.note: `TensorAlgebra R M` is defined directly.

annotations.label: Evaluation map

anchor.snippet: there is a canonical evaluation map

annotations.note: `contractLeft : V* ⊗ V →ₗ R` (and `contractRight`) is the canonical evaluation pairing.

annotations.label: Tensor contraction

anchor.snippet: there is a map, called tensor contraction

annotations.note: Tensor contraction is built from `contractLeft`/`contractRight`.

annotations.label: Coevaluation map

anchor.snippet: there is a canonical map in the other direction (called the coevaluation map

annotations.note: `coevaluation K V : K →ₗ V ⊗ V*` is the coevaluation map for a finite-dimensional vector space.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation via tensor product

anchor.snippet: may be naturally viewed as a module for the Lie algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib has Lie-module structure on tensor products in `Algebra.Lie.TensorProduct`, capturing the adjoint action on `V ⊗ V*`.

annotations.label: Canonical isomorphism for adjoint representation

anchor.snippet: There is a canonical isomorphism

annotations.note: The underlying linear iso `V* ⊗ V ≃ End V` is `dualTensorHomEquiv`; the Lie-module version specifically isn't stated.

annotations.label: Isomorphism U* ⊗ V ≅ Hom(U,V)

anchor.snippet: There is an isomorphism

annotations.note: `dualTensorHomEquiv : Module.Dual R M ⊗ N ≃ₗ M →ₗ N` for finite free modules.

annotations.label: Tensor-Hom adjunction

anchor.snippet: the tensor product is linked to the vector space of all linear maps

annotations.note: The Hom-Tensor adjunction is the equivalence `(M →ₗ N →ₗ P) ≃ (M ⊗ N →ₗ P)`.

annotations.label: Tensor product of modules over a commutative ring

anchor.section: Tensor products of modules over a ring
anchor.snippet: The tensor product of two modules

annotations.note: `TensorProduct R M N` for `R` a commutative semiring is exactly this.

annotations.label: Tensor product of modules over a non-commutative ring

anchor.section: Tensor products of modules over a ring
anchor.snippet: the tensor product can be defined even if the ring is non-commutative

annotations.note: Mathlib only defines the tensor product over commutative (semi)rings; bimodule/two-sided versions are handled via `CompatibleSMul` and `op`-modules but not as a distinct construction.

annotations.label: Canonical middle linear map

anchor.section: Tensor products of modules over a ring
anchor.snippet: is a middle linear map

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.mk R M N` is the canonical bilinear/middle-linear map `M →ₗ N →ₗ M ⊗ N`.

annotations.label: Tensor product of modules (non-commutative ring)

anchor.section: Tensor product of modules over a non-commutative ring
anchor.snippet: the tensor product of A and B is an abelian group defined by

annotations.note: Mathlib's `TensorProduct` covers the commutative case; for non-commutative rings one works through `Module.End` and opposite-ring modules.

annotations.label: Universal property for modules over non-commutative ring

anchor.section: Tensor product of modules over a non-commutative ring
anchor.snippet: The universal property can be stated as follows

annotations.note: Only the commutative-ring universal property is in Mathlib; the bimodule middle-linear version is not stated.

annotations.label: Bimodule structures on tensor product

anchor.section: Tensor product of modules over a non-commutative ring
anchor.snippet: we can give

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `leftAlgebra`/`rightAlgebra` instances for `A ⊗[R] B`; general non-commutative bimodule structures are not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Tensor product with Z/nZ

anchor.snippet: The tensor product with

annotations.note: No dedicated lemma computing `M ⊗ ZMod n ≃ M/nM` was found.

annotations.label: Tensor product as cokernel from presentation

anchor.snippet: given a presentation of some

annotations.note: Right exactness lemmas in `TensorProduct.RightExactness` give the cokernel description, though it's not packaged as a dedicated presentation lemma.

annotations.label: Tensor product is right exact, not left exact

anchor.snippet: the tensor product is a right exact functor

annotations.note: `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.RightExactness` establishes right-exactness; left-exactness failure is implicit (flatness needed).

annotations.label: Tensor product of algebras

anchor.snippet: the tensor product

annotations.note: `Algebra.TensorProduct` equips `A ⊗[R] B` with an algebra structure.

annotations.label: Tensor product of fields and Galois theory

anchor.snippet: A particular example is when A and B are fields containing a common subfield

annotations.note: `Algebra.TensorProduct.isField_of_isAlgebraic` covers when the tensor product of algebraic field extensions is a field; full Galois-theoretic decomposition isn't stated.

annotations.label: Eigenconfiguration of a tensor

anchor.snippet: The fixed points of nonlinear maps are the eigenvectors of tensors

annotations.note: No formalization of tensor eigenconfigurations in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological tensor product of Hilbert spaces

anchor.snippet: There is an analogous operation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Hilbert-space (completed) tensor product.

annotations.label: Tensor product of representations / algebras

anchor.snippet: The tensor product of such algebras is described by the Littlewood

annotations.note: Tensor products of representations exist in `RepresentationTheory`, but Littlewood–Richardson decomposition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Tensor product of algebraic field extensions

anchor.snippet: their tensor product over the base field is again algebraic over the base field

annotations.note: `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.tensorProduct` shows `R' ⊗[R] S` is algebraic over `R'` when `S` is algebraic over `R`.

annotations.label: Tensor product of multilinear forms

anchor.snippet: their tensor product is the multilinear form

annotations.note: `MultilinearMap.domCoprod` (and `domCoprodDep`) produces the tensor product of multilinear maps.

annotations.label: Exterior product / exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: the exterior product

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra R M` is the exterior algebra as a quotient of `TensorAlgebra`.

annotations.label: Symmetric algebra

anchor.snippet: The symmetric algebra is constructed in a similar manner

annotations.note: `SymmetricAlgebra R M := RingQuot (SymRel R M)` is the symmetric algebra.
